title: Pre-Call Briefing
description: Explore meeting preparation briefings including company research, value hypotheses, and discovery questions, then review your briefing history.

steps.narration: The Meeting Prep page helps you prepare for upcoming calls. Right now there are no briefings yet, so you see the empty state along with the shared assistant banner explaining how to get started.
The shared assistant banner explains that the docked deal assistant follows you across overview, briefing, and touch pages. You can ask for tailored prep, save notes, or request similar case matches.

callout.text: Use the assistant for briefing work

steps.narration: A briefing is now being generated. You can see it appear in the Previous Briefings section with a running status badge while the AI pulls company data and analyzes the deal context.
The running briefing card shows it's targeting a VP of Technology buyer role for an initial discovery call. This context shapes the AI's research focus and the talking points it generates.
The briefing is complete. Click the card to expand it and see the full generated content. The status badge now shows completed with the date and buyer role.

The company research section shows Meridian Dynamics as a mid-market technology consulting firm with over two hundred and fifty employees. They recently expanded their cloud practice and partnered with Snowflake.

Scrolling down to the value hypotheses. These are tailored talking points: legacy ERP modernization could save forty percent on integration costs, and a unified data platform would reduce handoff time by sixty percent.
Each value hypothesis is specific and actionable, grounded in the deal context. The cloud migration path is aligned with Meridian's Q3 growth projections for containerized architecture.

The discovery questions give you conversation starters tailored to Meridian's situation. What's driving the transformation timeline, how are teams sharing customer data, and what does Q3 success look like.
Now let's look at the briefing history. Multiple briefings have been generated for this deal, each tailored to a different buyer role and meeting context.

The history shows three past briefings. The first targeted the VP of Technology for discovery, the second a Director of Engineering for a technical deep dive, and the third a follow-up on proposal review.
Each briefing card is expandable to review the full content. With the deal assistant at the bottom, you can always ask for more preparation or save notes right from this page.
